# Bombelli 1987 Causal Set Simulated Annealing Revival

> A Python 3.12 revival and empirical audit of Luca Bombelli's 1987 Pascal program
> for embedding causal sets into Minkowski spacetime by simulated annealing.

This project preserves, reimplements, and empirically audits a 1987 piece of discrete
quantum gravity software. The original program (Luca Bombelli's PhD thesis, Syracuse
University, 1987) attempted to find Minkowski coordinates for causal sets by minimizing
a penalty energy function via simulated annealing. The revival includes a faithful
Python 3.12 port of the Pascal source, reproducible CPU experiments, ensemble statistics,
order-theoretic diagnostics, and benchmark tables. A companion research program called
SORKIN-2 studies the algorithmic recoverability of the historical annealer on known-truth
causal sets.

Author: Jose Ignacio Martin Gandul
License: MIT
DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20307736


## Pages on this site

- [Project overview](https://nacho09021973.github.io/bombelli/): Landing page with benchmark tables, visualizations, and project context
- [Research note](https://nacho09021973.github.io/bombelli/reviving-bombelli-1987-causal-set-code.html): Short paper — revival methodology, main findings, and limitations


## Full resources

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/nacho09021973/bombelli): All source code, benchmark data, and documentation
- [Full paper (Markdown)](https://github.com/nacho09021973/bombelli/blob/main/paper/bombelli_revival_2026.md): Complete research note
- [Zenodo archive](https://zenodo.org/records/20307736): Citable DOI archive
- [Citation metadata (CFF)](https://github.com/nacho09021973/bombelli/blob/main/CITATION.cff): Machine-readable citation
- [cones.py](https://github.com/nacho09021973/bombelli/blob/main/cones.py): Python 3.12 port of the Pascal annealer
- [validation_suite.py](https://github.com/nacho09021973/bombelli/blob/main/validation_suite.py): Order-theoretic verification tools


## Technical context

**Causal set theory**: A candidate theory of quantum gravity in which spacetime is
fundamentally discrete. Events form a locally finite partially ordered set (poset)
where the partial order encodes causal relations. Causal set theory was developed
by Bombelli, Lee, Meyer, and Sorkin (1987).

**Causal relation**: Event i causally precedes event j (written i ≺ j) if i is in
the causal past of j. In Minkowski spacetime, i ≺ j iff Δt ≥ 0 and
(Δt)² ≥ (Δx₁)² + (Δx₂)² + ... (timelike or null separation with i earlier than j).

**Bombelli energy function**: A penalty that scores how well a set of Minkowski
coordinates reproduces a given causal order. Zero energy means the coordinates
induce exactly the target causal order. The energy was defined in Bombelli's 1987
PhD thesis and encodes missing relations (pairs that should be causally related but
aren't in the recovered geometry) and extra relations (spurious causal links).

**Simulated annealing**: The optimization algorithm used to minimize the Bombelli
energy. Starts at high temperature (many uphill moves accepted) and cools according
to T_k = T₀ · γ^k. The historical schedule uses T₀ = 100, cooling factor γ = 0.9,
35 cooling blocks with 100 reconfigure steps per block.

**SORKIN-2**: The research continuation within this project. Studies whether the
historical annealer can algorithmically recover known-truth causal realizations.
Distinct from physical embeddability: a causal set may be embeddable in Minkowski
spacetime even if the historical annealer fails to find the embedding.

**exact_match**: The primary recovery criterion in SORKIN-2. True iff the causal
order induced by the annealer's final coordinates reproduces the target causal order
exactly (zero missing relations, zero extra relations).


## Key experimental findings (SORKIN-2)

- N ≤ 6 (chain, antichain, Minkowski 6-event sprinkling at seed 1959): the historical
  annealer recovers exact causal order under the original schedule.
- N = 12, seed 1962 (Minkowski sprinkling), historical schedule T₀=100 γ=0.9:
  FAILS. Only 1 of 19 target causal relations induced; final energy ≈ 16.8.
- N = 12, same input, tuned schedule T₀=180 γ=0.8: SUCCEEDS. All 19 of 19 target
  causal relations induced; final energy ≈ 7×10⁻⁶. exact_match = true.
- 2×2 schedule grid (T₀ ∈ {100,180} × γ ∈ {0.8,0.9}): T₀=100 γ=0.8 → success;
  T₀=180 γ=0.9 → failure. The cooling factor γ is the dominant bottleneck, not T₀.
- Geometric failure mode for N=12 historical run: 16 of 19 missing relations are
  SPACELIKE (correct temporal order but insufficient R-spread); 3 are WRONG_ORDER.
  The historical final configuration has R-span ≈ 29 versus ≈ 90 for the tuned run.
- The failure belongs to the historical protocol, not to the Bombelli energy function
  or to the causal set (which was constructed by sprinkling known Minkowski coordinates).


## Scope and limitations

- This project is a preservation and audit, not a claim about quantum gravity or
  manifoldlikeness.
- Annealer success or failure is a statement about algorithmic recoverability, not
  about physical embeddability of the causal set.
- Low final Bombelli energy is necessary but not sufficient for exact causal order
  recovery. The antichain case (N=4) has nonzero final energy but exact_match=true.
- All SORKIN-2 results are for single seeds on constructed (known-truth) inputs.
  No generalization to arbitrary causal sets is claimed.
- The Bombelli annealer is not a manifoldlikeness detector.


## Citation

Jose Ignacio Martin Gandul (2026). *Thirty-Seven Years of Simulated Annealing on a
Causal Set: A Revival of Bombelli (1987) with 2026 Tools*. Zenodo.
https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20307736
